sections.heading: RT
sections.content: Radiation therapy is not recommended as a primary therapy for peritoneal mesothelioma. Its role is limited and selective, primarily for palliation of specific symptoms. Primary systemic disease within the peritoneal cavity is not amenable to effective radiation therapy due to the diffuse nature of the disease and risk of toxicity to surrounding organs. Radiation may be considered for palliation of localized, symptomatic problems such as painful bone metastases or focal tumor deposits causing pain or bleeding.
